channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Iran war deal close as Strait of Hormuz tensions linger
item.description: Hopes for peace grew after Trump said a deal could be signed this weekend, though Tehran said no final decision has been made on a pact. The strategic Strait of Hormuz remains a key sticking point in negotiations.

item.title: US and EU near deadline on $11.5 billion tariffs in decades-long aircraft dispute
item.description: Washington and Brussels have yet to decide whether to continue suspending or reimpose tariffs on $11.5 billion of goods in a dispute over aircraft subsidies, with their truce expiring within days.

item.title: Trump says US "ended the war with Iran" but Tehran has yet to confirm deal
item.description: The US president said a peace deal could be signed within days and he may attend a signing ceremony. Iranian officials denied any final agreement had been reached, calling reports speculative.

item.title: UK Defense Minister Resigns Over Defense Funding
item.description: UK Armed Forces Minister Al Carns resigned in protest over lack of defense funding, following Defense Secretary John Healey's earlier resignation. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a political crisis as two defense ministers quit within days.

item.title: Philippines Defense Chief Vows to Press on Despite Chinese Sanctions
item.description: Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro vowed to continue his duties despite China sanctioning him for "erroneous remarks," calling out China's "wickedness" as tensions escalate over South China Sea disputes.

item.title: UAE Sends Military Planes to South Korea for Missile Defense Transport
item.description: UAE military transport aircraft arrived in South Korea as part of ongoing defense cooperation between the two nations, highlighting deepening military ties in the Indo-Pacific region.

item.title: ECB signals option to raise rates again in July after insurance hike
item.description: European Central Bank policymaker Joachim Nagel said the ECB is keeping its options open for July after raising interest rates for the first time in nearly three years amid inflation pressures from the Iran war energy shock.

item.title: UK lags European rivals in curbing influx of cheap parcels from China
item.description: British retailers are pushing for taxes on low-value Chinese parcels as the UK falls behind EU countries in addressing the flood of cheap imports that undermine domestic businesses.

item.title: World chess body suspends Russia after sports tribunal ruling
item.description: The International Chess Federation (FIDE) suspended Russia following a sports tribunal ruling, marking another blow to Russian sports amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths in powerful Philippine earthquake
item.description: Regular disaster preparedness drills in the Philippines helped minimize casualties when a powerful earthquake struck, officials said, highlighting the importance of emergency training.

item.title: US Plans Major Cuts to Fighter Jets and Warships for NATO Operations in Europe
item.description: The US military is planning significant reductions in fighter jets and warships dedicated to NATO operations in Europe, according to the New York Times, amid questions about future US commitment to European defense.

item.title: British Defence Minister John Healey Resigns Over Spending Dispute
item.description: UK Defence Secretary John Healey resigned over government unwillingness to increase military spending, criticizing the Labour government's defense budget priorities amid global security challenges.

item.title: China Cancels High-Level Diplomatic Meetings With EU in Beijing
item.description: China abruptly cancelled high-level diplomatic meetings with the European Union in Beijing, the Financial Times reported, amid growing tensions over trade and geopolitical issues.

item.title: Fuel Stations in Crimea Run Dry After Ukrainian Drone Strikes
item.description: Fuel stations in Russian-occupied Crimea are running dry after Ukrainian drone attacks targeted supply lines, disrupting the peninsula's fuel distribution network.

item.title: Geneva Steps Up Security for 50,000-Strong G7 Protest
item.description: Geneva is on high alert with heightened security measures as 50,000 protesters are expected to demonstrate during the G7 summit hosted by France this weekend.

item.title: Japan-South Korea Summit Aims to Deepen Cooperation Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Japan and South Korea are working to strengthen bilateral ties as both nations navigate an increasingly volatile regional security environment amid North Korean threats and great-power competition.

item.title: France, UK, Germany Envoys Meet Russian Foreign Ministry Officials
item.description: Envoys from France, the UK, and Germany met with Russian Foreign Ministry officials in Moscow, amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to address European security concerns.

item.title: Germany and France Scrap FCAS Fighter Jet Project
item.description: Germany and France have scrapped their joint Future Combat Air System (FCAS) fighter jet project, dealing a major blow to European defense cooperation and industrial partnership.

item.title: South Korea ex-President Yoon sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone plot
item.description: A Seoul court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for ordering drone flights over North Korea to create a pretext for his failed December 2024 martial law declaration.

item.title: Trump says "great" Iran settlement will reopen Strait of Hormuz within days
item.description: President Trump announced a "great settlement" with Iran would be signed within days, saying the strategic Strait of Hormuz would reopen as part of the agreement to end hostilities.

item.title: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups meet in France to push for two-state solution
item.description: Israeli and Palestinian civil society organizations met in France to advocate for a two-state solution, as prospects for the long-sought peace framework continue to dim amid ongoing regional turmoil.

item.title: Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, says deal "approved" after key points agreed
item.description: President Trump cancelled planned military strikes on Iran and announced that key points of a peace agreement had been approved, marking a potential de-escalation in the conflict.

item.title: Ukraine War Has Lasted Longer Than World War I
item.description: The war in Ukraine has now lasted longer than World War I, reaching 1,569 days of conflict with no clear end in sight, marking a grim milestone in modern European history.

item.title: US and Iran haggle over frozen funds as interim deal nears
item.description: Iran and the United States are negotiating the release of frozen Iranian funds as part of efforts to reach an interim deal to end hostilities, sources told Reuters.

item.title: China Sanctions Philippine Defense Secretary Over South China Sea Remarks
item.description: China sanctioned Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro for what it described as "repeated erroneous remarks" about China, escalating diplomatic tensions over South China Sea disputes.
